titlestmt.titleproper: Advocate Research and Education Foundation records7310
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Phil McCray

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-01-08 18:23:07 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Advocate Research and Education Foundation records
did.physdesc: Correspondence, financial records, registration files, topical seminar background material, "facilitator information sheets," audio and video cassettes, slides.
did.abstract: The collection pertains chiefly to the Advocate Experience, an EST-derived seminar for lesbians and gays founded by David B. Goodstein. The contains various materials from 1978 to 1981 of the Advocate Research and Education Foundation Board and the Advocate Experience staff, and manuals pertaining to the Advocate Experience and the Outrageous Experience (a seminar for instructors).

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: In the 1970s, David B. Goodstein created the Advocate Research and Education Foundation, a non-profit structure through which to conduct the "Advocate Experience." Initially the Experience was a one-weekend seminar similar to Werner Erhard's EST, only targeted at a gay audience. The foundation was eventually combined with the Mariposa Education and Research Foundation.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The collection is divided into four series: Advocate Research and Education Foundation and Advocate Experience Correspondence and Memos' and Alphabetical Subject File; Facilitator Information Sheets; and an Audio and Video cassette series. The Facilitator Information Sheets are restricted until 2041.

defitem.label: Series III. Advocate Experience Facilitator Information Sheets
Series IV. Advocate Experience Audio and Video Cassettes and Slides

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Advocate Research and Education Foundation/Advocate Experience Records, #7310. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Series IV. Advocate Experience Audio and Video Cassettes and Slides
Sixteen videotapes pertaining to the Advocate Experience, including training sessions, graduates sharing the experience, and David B. Goodstein presentations
Twenty audiocassettes pertaining to the Advocate Experience, including seminar sessions, Opening Space session, disco music, and "Ethel Merman Single"
Approximately 90 slides pertaining to the Advocate Experience
